channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. military says it intercepted Iranian attacks on 3 Navy ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. military also says that it "targeted Iranian military facilities responsible for attacking U.S. forces." The exchange occurred Thursday as U.S. Navy destroyers transited the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Central Command said in a social media post.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military also says that it "targeted Iranian military facilities responsible for attacking U.S. forces." The exchange occurred Thursday as U.S. Navy destroyers transited the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Central Command said in a social media post.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat/ISNA)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The show still goes on: Federal employees are honored after a tumultuous year
item.description: Amid tumultuous times for federal workers, a team of USDA researchers is being honored with the Service to America medal. Their work over decades has helped to revolutionize the dairy industry.
item.content:encoded: Amid tumultuous times for federal workers, a team of USDA researchers is being honored with the Service to America medal. Their work over decades has helped to revolutionize the dairy industry.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: New Mexico opens investigations into possible crimes at Epstein's old ranch
item.description: New Mexico officials have re-opened an old criminal investigation and formed a legislative committee to look into allegations of abuse at the 10,000-acre ranch owned by Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded: New Mexico officials have re-opened an old criminal investigation and formed a legislative committee to look into allegations of abuse at the 10,000-acre ranch owned by Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: DANIEL MONTANO

item.title: She helps families in need. As gas and grocery prices rise, she needs help, too
item.description: She has spent decades helping others struggling to make ends meet. Now the rising cost of gas and groceries has left Dalene Basden feeling the strain herself.
item.content:encoded:
She has spent decades helping others struggling to make ends meet. Now the rising cost of gas and groceries has left Dalene Basden feeling the strain herself.

(Image credit: Tovia Smith/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: With legal briefs in, Supreme Court weighs telehealth access for the abortion pill
item.description: After an appeals court tried to end telemedicine access to mifepristone, one of the two pills used in a medication abortion, the Supreme Court stepped in. Here's what's happened and what's to come.
item.content:encoded:
After an appeals court tried to end telemedicine access to mifepristone, one of the two pills used in a medication abortion, the Supreme Court stepped in. Here's what's happened and what's to come.

(Image credit: Jonathan Bachman)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: A judge unsealed Epstein's purported 2019 suicide note. More documents could follow
item.description: At the request of the New York Times, a judge unsealed a goodbye note that Jeffrey Epstein's former cellmate says he found after the convicted sex offender's first suicide attempt in July 2019.
item.content:encoded:
At the request of the New York Times, a judge unsealed a goodbye note that Jeffrey Epstein's former cellmate says he found after the convicted sex offender's first suicide attempt in July 2019.

(Image credit: Jon Elswick)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Campaign staffers tell NPR they make 'thousands' betting on their own candidates
item.description: Campaign staffers are turning private polling data into personal paydays. They describe the election prediction market as a "Wild West" for staffers.
item.content:encoded: Campaign staffers are turning private polling data into personal paydays. They describe the election prediction market as a "Wild West" for staffers.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: Making a podcast helped one family talk about aging, dementia and death
item.description: This year's winner in NPR's College Podcast Challenge is a letter to a grandparent that grapples with health issues including dementia. It's the story of a family learning to talk about hard things.
item.content:encoded:
This year's winner in NPR's College Podcast Challenge is a letter to a grandparent that grapples with health issues including dementia. It's the story of a family learning to talk about hard things.

(Image credit: Matthew Coughlin for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Federal authorities arrest 18 for alleged drug distribution around Los Angeles park
item.description: The area, called MacArthur Park, is a densely populated immigrant neighborhood west of downtown LA where federal immigration authorities and the National Guard made a brief show of force last summer.
item.content:encoded:
The area, called MacArthur Park, is a densely populated immigrant neighborhood west of downtown LA where federal immigration authorities and the National Guard made a brief show of force last summer.

(Image credit: Jae C. Hong)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump's counterterrorism strategy makes targeting drug cartels the top priority
item.description: President Trump has signed off on a new U.S. counterterrorism strategy that sets eliminating drug cartels in the Western Hemisphere as the administration's highest priority.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has signed off on a new U.S. counterterrorism strategy that sets eliminating drug cartels in the Western Hemisphere as the administration's highest priority.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
